signals.reasoning: The live SOL short is back to worsening as a real loser after fully round-tripping from peak profit, retains none of peak PnL, still has no stop, and liquidation distance remains under 1%. This is a failed short that should be cleaned up, not defended.

BTC remains firm on the day, which directly works against defending the SOL short, but this is still a position-management cycle so the right move is flattening bad risk rather than forcing a new BTC decision.
ETH is participating in the same constructive tape, so it does not offer a cleaner immediate action than closing the failed SOL short.
ORDI is already well into rebound territory and does not justify attention over simply getting flat.
MERL has strong recovery structure, but that makes it more of a chase risk than a disciplined next action in position management.
BOME is just another noisy rebound name and not a better immediate decision than closing the failed SOL short.
FARTCOIN has shifted from earlier bearish pressure into a more constructive rebound, which weakens the case for fresh downside thinking.
VVV is strong but already extended enough that it belongs on a watchlist rather than as a fresh rotation while an urgent bad short is still open.
TST remains too extended and crowded to justify touching here.
ZEREBRO still looks more like noisy short-covering than a clean fresh opportunity, so it is not where disciplined attention should go.
